mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, which systematically drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns fail to track PAYEMS growth trends, yielding a negative or null Rank IC over consecutive 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: When real sales of goods and services exceed 510 billion dollars, demand saturation triggers inventory corrections and margin compression, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where RSAFS crosses above 510000, or the signal loses predictive power during low-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index reading above 108.0 confirms manufacturing capacity utilization has recovered to expansionary levels, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO sustains values above 108.0, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a declining federal funds rate alongside a stable or falling unemployment rate creates a goldilocks environment that compresses risk premiums and drives strong positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or positive returns fail to materialize when both conditions are met simultaneously over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and decelerating nonfarm payrolls signals a shift toward construction-led growth with weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as cyclical earnings disappoint.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show positive correlation with the HOUST-PAYEMS divergence, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regressions over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation readings exceed 253.0, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during inflationary regimes.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 253.0, or the signal fails to predict direction during periods of rising price indices.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 18.7 trillion chained dollars combined with elevated industrial output confirms a high-growth macro regime that systematically supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns turn negative or flat when GDPC1 exceeds 18700.0, or the high-growth regime fails to generate a positive Rank IC in out-of-sample testing.
